name: Session 2026-04-07

messages.content:
Using `github:yeet` for the commit/push boundary, with the PR-opening parts skipped because you explicitly asked to push `main`. I’ll re-check the tree, reload the local rules, then scan source-wide for concrete optimization fixes only.

The local publish skill assumes a PR flow and `gh`, but this environment does not have `gh` and your target is direct `main`; I’ll keep using `git` directly and will not create an empty commit. The repo is currently clean for source files, with only the known `.cozter` local files modified.

The first broad pass found no explicit hack/backward-compat markers that are actionable. A few optimization candidates are worth inspecting closely now: set/map membership checks in ECS scheduling and Vulkan state tracking, plus stale-handle cleanup paths in deformable runtime code.

I found one real copy-avoidance opportunity in command-line parsing: parsed string options are currently validated, then copied into `CookOptions`, even though the parsed owner is discarded immediately. I’m also tightening a few hot membership checks from `count()` to `find()` so robin-map/set lookups stay to the minimal operation.

The edits are scoped to three source files. `apply_patch` introduced mixed line endings in those files, so I’m normalizing them back to the repo’s CRLF convention before building.

The changed files are back to CRLF and `git diff --check` is clean. I’m doing another pass now for similar copy-then-discard patterns and remaining container lookup opportunities before running the full validation matrix.

A second pass found one more concrete optimization in shader metadata parsing: one list path already precomputes variant string size, while the default-variant parser appends the same shape without reserving. I’m aligning those paths to avoid incremental string growth during shader cook.

The aligned-float sweep did not show a safe conversion opportunity: persistent scene/GPU structs that can be aligned already use `Float4`/`alignas(Float4)`, while asset payload structs stay on `Float*U` storage for compact serialized layouts. I’m moving to the standard Linux validation matrix now.

`dbg` build completed, including the normal two-root asset cook during the build. I’m running `ctest -C dbg` next, then I’ll repeat build/test for `fin` and `opt`.

`fin` build passed. I’m running its test suite now, then `opt`.

`opt` build also passed with the two-root asset cook. I’m running the final CTest pass and then the isolated resource-cooker smokes before staging the source-only commit.

All three isolated two-root cooker smokes passed with an empty `--asset-type` argument. I’m doing one final source scan and diff review now; if nothing else is concrete, I’ll commit only the source files and push `main`.

Final scan found no additional concrete source change: remaining `.count()` calls are duration/entity-count APIs, and the remaining `assign(data,size)` sites either preserve custom allocator/view boundaries or exact external shader entry text. I’m fetching `origin/main`, then I’ll stage only the four source files.

Only the four source files are staged; the `.cozter` files remain local and unstaged. The staged diff passes whitespace checks, so I’m committing and pushing to `main`.

Implemented and pushed `0cf21eb` to `main`: `Optimize command line and lookup paths`.

Changed:
- Moved parsed resource-cooker command-line strings into `CookOptions` instead of copying discarded parsed values.
- Reserved default shader variant string capacity before appending list entries.
- Replaced remaining robin set/map membership `count()` checks in ECS scheduling and Vulkan state tracking with direct `find()` checks.

Validated:
- `cmake --build __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 --config dbg|fin|opt`
- `ctest --test-dir __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 -C dbg|fin|opt --output-on-failure`
- isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt`

Final source scan did not find another concrete optimization to patch. Workspace is clean except the pre-existing unstaged `.cozter` files.

now check the whole sources for optimizable code and fix them.

if you couldn't find more to fix, commit and push to main

Using `github:yeet` for the commit/push boundary, but I’ll keep the flow aligned with your direct-`main` request and avoid PR-only steps. I’ll re-check the tree, reload the local rules, then run another source-wide optimization pass and only change concrete findings.

The publish skill expects a PR workflow and `gh`, but this repository workflow is direct push to `main`; I’ll still use its safety rules: inspect scope, stage only intended source changes, validate before push, and avoid empty commits. The working tree is source-clean right now except for the existing `.cozter` files.

I’ve reloaded the local standard. The next pass is performance-focused: avoidable value copies, repeated lookups, missing reserves where counts are known, and allocations in transient hot paths.

I found a real remaining optimization in the asset cooker registry: it eagerly formats and sorts the “available cookers” string even on the normal success path where no diagnostic needs it. I’m going to make that diagnostic string lazy and reserve the final joined text size when it is actually built.

The patch landed, and I caught mixed line endings immediately afterward. I’m normalizing this file back to the repo’s CRLF convention before doing anything else.

The concrete change is now CRLF-clean: diagnostic formatting is no longer on the success path, and the diagnostic string reserves its final size when built. I’m continuing the pass now to see whether there are more source-level optimization candidates worth changing.

Another concrete success-path allocation showed up in material variant normalization: the context label string is built even when the material requests the default variant and returns before validation. I’ll move that string construction onto the non-default path and avoid the two intermediate `AString` copies.

I found a hotter filesystem path too: every volume segment filename hash builds a temporary formatted string just to hash it. I’m replacing that with incremental FNV hashing over the same text parts, preserving the exact `"volume_index.vol"` seed text without allocating.

The filesystem staging helpers had the same pattern in a shared path: `StringFormat` was used to build fixed prefix/suffix names. I’m replacing those with reserved append sequences, and tightening the volume cooker stage-token assembly to avoid intermediate formatted strings.

I’ve exhausted the fixed-format string allocation candidates that were still concrete and local. The source diff is limited to six files; I’m moving into validation now, starting with the configured Linux build matrix.

The `dbg` build completed and the integrated cook step still succeeds. I’m running `ctest -C dbg` now, then I’ll repeat build/test for `fin` and `opt`.

`fin` is still building cleanly through the changed filesystem, asset cooker, Vulkan, and shader-cooker objects. No compile regressions have shown up; I’m letting the rest of the target graph finish.

`opt` is compiling the changed areas now; this is the last configuration build before the isolated cooker smokes and final source scan.

All three build/test configurations are clean so far. I’m running the isolated two-root cooker smokes now with `impl/assets` and `Testbed/assets`, using an empty `--asset-type` so it exercises auto selection without polluting the repo.

The isolated cooker smokes passed for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt`. I’m doing one final source scan and diff hygiene pass now; if that stays clean, I’ll commit only the source files and push `main`.

The final scans found no further concrete optimization target: only wrapper macro definitions for CRT use, comment-only “temporary” text, and value range loops over scalar/pointer types. I’m staging just the six source files now, leaving `.cozter` untouched.

Committed the source-only optimization pass as `57bd381`. I’m pushing `main` now, then I’ll verify the final branch state.

Pushed `main` with commit `57bd381` (`Optimize staging and cache name formatting`).

Changed:
- Removed eager asset-cooker diagnostic formatting on success paths.
- Replaced fixed `StringFormat`/hex-name construction with reserved append paths.
- Avoided a temporary allocation when hashing volume segment filenames.
- Reused `AppendHexU64` instead of duplicate hex formatting code.
- Delayed material variant context-string construction until it is actually needed.

Validated:
- `cmake --build __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 --config dbg|fin|opt`
- `ctest --test-dir __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 -C dbg|fin|opt --output-on-failure`
- isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes for `impl/assets` + `Testbed/assets` in `dbg|fin|opt` with empty `--asset-type`

Final source tree is clean and synced with `origin/main`; only the existing local `.cozter` files remain modified.

now check the whole sources for optimizable code and fix them.

if you couldn't find more to fix, commit and push to main

Using `github:yeet` for the commit/push boundary, with the PR-specific parts skipped because you asked for a direct `main` push. I’ll re-check the tree, reload the local standard, scan source-wide for concrete optimization candidates, patch only evidence-backed issues, then validate before any commit.

The working tree is still `main...origin/main` with only `.cozter` local edits. I’ve reloaded the standard; next I’m separating broad scans from manual inspection so I can avoid churn and only patch candidates that are actually optimization wins.

The broad scan is mostly expected cases: small handles copied by value, explicit reserves already present, and wrapper macro definitions. I’m now checking higher-signal clusters where a small change could remove repeated lookups or allocation growth in hot paths without changing behavior.

I found a few concrete hot-path cleanups: stale-resource tracking is paid every frame even when there is nothing stale to compare, and one material render-path logger does an avoidable double map lookup. I’m going to patch those narrowly, plus a tiny descriptor lookup insertion cleanup that preserves the existing behavior.

`apply_patch` left the touched source files with mixed line endings under the repo’s `eol=crlf` rule. I’m normalizing only those modified files back to CRLF before continuing.

The second scan did not turn up heavier value-copy loops or direct CRT/console violations outside wrapper definitions. The remaining indexed loops are either scalar, mutation-sensitive, or already reserve-backed, so I’m moving to validation on the patched files.

The source diff is four files, and `.cozter` remains the only unrelated dirt. I’m starting the required Linux matrix now: build and `ctest -C dbg`, then `fin`, then `opt`, followed by the two-root cooker smokes.

`dbg` build and `ctest -C dbg` passed. I’m continuing with `fin` and then `opt` before touching git.

`fin` and `opt` builds and tests also passed. I’m running the isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes now with an empty asset-type argument, as required for this repo’s auto cooker selection.

All validation passed, and the source files are back to CRLF with `git diff --check` clean. I’m staging only the four source files now, leaving the existing `.cozter` edits out of the commit.

Committed the source-only optimization changes as `ccd52c0`. I’m pushing `main` to `origin` now; the `.cozter` files remain unstaged local edits.

Implemented and pushed `ccd52c0 Optimize runtime resource tracking` to `origin/main`.

Changed:
- Deferred active/stale hash tracking when no runtime cache exists in [deformable_runtime_mesh_cache.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/ecs_graphics/deformable_runtime_mesh_cache.cpp:263).
- Deferred deformer live-handle tracking when no runtime resources exist in [deformer_system.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/ecs_graphics/deformer_system.cpp:409).
- Collapsed material render-path logging to one map lookup in [renderer_system.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/impl/ecs_graphics/renderer_system.cpp:3010).
- Replaced descriptor heap lookup `operator[]` assignment with `insert_or_assign` in [vulkan_resource_bindings.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_resource_bindings.cpp:1907).

Validated:
- `cmake --build __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 --config dbg|fin|opt`
- `ctest --test-dir __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 -C dbg|fin|opt --output-on-failure`
- isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt`

Final status: `main` matches `origin/main`; only the existing local `.cozter` files remain modified and uncommitted.

now check the whole sources for optimizable code and fix them.

if you couldn't find more to fix, commit and push to main

Using `github:yeet` for the commit/push boundary, but I’ll keep it aligned with your direct `main` workflow and skip PR-specific steps. I’m going to re-check the tree and source rules first, then run another broad optimization pass and only patch concrete, defensible findings.

The tree is source-clean apart from the known `.cozter` local files. I’ve reloaded the style rules, and I’m now doing high-signal scans for repeated associative lookups, avoidable container growth, and value copies across non-`3rd_parties` sources.

One concrete Vulkan hot-path pattern is showing up: automatic resource state transitions do a permanent-state lookup, then the state query repeats that lookup, and tracking repeats it again. I’m checking that path against the public tracking APIs before editing so the external behavior stays unchanged.

I found a second concrete ECS path: `World::tryGetComponent` does `has()` and then `get()`, and `ComponentPool::remove()` also validates once then re-derives the dense index. I’m going to add a single-index lookup helper in the component pool and route the hot callers through it.

The first edits are in: ECS `tryGetComponent` and removal now share one dense-index lookup, and Vulkan transitions now keep the public permanent-state behavior while avoiding repeated permanent checks before tracking. I’m normalizing the touched source files back to CRLF before building.

The current diff is narrow and CRLF-clean. I’m doing one more pass over string/name and cooker paths now, since those are easy places to accidentally allocate or sort more than needed.

There is another concrete allocation win in `NamePool::store`: existing names canonicalize into a temporary string before the pool even checks whether the canonical spelling is already stored. I’m changing the hit path to compare against stored canonical text without allocating, while keeping collision checks intact.

I found and patched one more source-wide optimization: repeated `NamePool::store` hits now avoid allocating a canonical string and compare against the stored canonical text directly. I’m moving into the Linux `dbg/fin/opt` verification matrix now.

`dbg` is compiling and caught two expected `[[nodiscard]]` warnings from the new Vulkan helper calls in the public query wrappers. I’ll let this build finish, then I’ll patch those calls to consume the boolean explicitly and rerun the matrix.

I patched the warning cleanly by making the public Vulkan state queries return through the helper’s boolean result. Now I’m rerunning the `dbg` build incrementally before continuing to `fin` and `opt`.

`dbg` now rebuilds cleanly. I’m running the `fin` build next, then `opt`, then the explicit `ctest -C dbg|fin|opt` runs and the two-root cooker smokes.

All three build configurations completed cleanly. I’m running the explicit CTest pass for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt` from `__cmake/build/linux-clang-x64`.

The full `ctest` matrix passed. I’m doing the required isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes next, using the empty asset-type argument for auto selection.

The two-root cooker smokes passed for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt`. I’m doing the final diff/status checks now, then I’ll stage only the source changes and push `main`.

Only the five intended source files are staged; the `.cozter` files remain unstaged local state. I’m committing this as a small optimization sweep and then pushing `main`.

Implemented and pushed `eb5c5cd` (`Optimize hot path lookups`) to `origin/main`.

Changes made:
- [global/name_pool.h](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/global/name_pool.h): avoids canonical string allocation on existing `NamePool::store` hits.
- [core/ecs/component.h](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/ecs/component.h) and [core/ecs/world.h](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/ecs/world.h): shares one dense-index lookup for `tryGetComponent` and removal paths.
- [core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_state_tracking.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_state_tracking.cpp): avoids repeated permanent-state checks in Vulkan transition tracking.

Verified:
- `cmake --build --preset linux-clang-dbg|fin|opt`
- `ctest -C dbg|fin|opt --output-on-failure`
- two-root `resource_cooker` smokes for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt`

Working tree is synced with `origin/main`; only the pre-existing `.cozter` local files remain unstaged.

check if there's any backward compability code and remove them.
once you've done your job, commit and push

Using `github:yeet` for the commit/push boundary, with PR-specific pieces skipped because you asked for a direct push. I’ll first reload the workflow rules and repo status, then scan non-`3rd_parties` sources for explicit compatibility shims and remove only confirmed dead/backward-compat paths.

The source tree is clean except for the pre-existing `.cozter` files, and we are on `main`. I’m starting with text markers first because compatibility code is usually labeled as legacy/deprecated/compat, then I’ll inspect any candidates manually before touching source.

The explicit compatibility markers only hit helper notes and one ordinary “old segments” cleanup log, not removable source code. I’m broadening the search now to versioned loaders, aliases, and alternate-key parsing, because those often carry compatibility behavior without saying “legacy.”

The second pass confirmed the known removed APIs are not present: no `executeCommandList` singular API, no global `Mutex` alias, and no old math compatibility surface. Two candidate areas remain worth reading: explicit rejection of removed metadata fields and filesystem cleanup around old segment files.

The metadata rejection paths are not compatibility support: they fail fast on removed fields, and one of them is a required guard for virtual path overrides. A more concrete cleanup candidate appeared in `AssetRef`: it still exposes text-based construction/setter overloads even though current callers already use typed `Name`/`AssetRef` flow. I’m checking that before editing.

I found one removable compatibility/convenience surface: `AssetRef<T>` still accepts `CompactString` directly and has a matching `set(CompactString)` helper, but source-wide usage has already moved to `Name` or explicit `AssetRef<T>`. I’m removing those unused raw-text entry points so asset references stay typed at the boundary.

The edit is intentionally narrow: only the unused `CompactString` constructor and setter were removed from `core/assets/asset_ref.h`. I’m checking the diff, whitespace, and line endings now before building.

`apply_patch` left mixed line endings in the touched header, which this repo explicitly disallows. I’m normalizing that file back to CRLF before doing the build checks.

The remaining `.cozter` changes are still pre-existing and outside the commit scope. The source diff is just the `AssetRef` compatibility overload removal; I’m going to run the established Linux build and test matrix before staging it.

The `dbg` build is underway; it had to regenerate a fair amount because Ninja recovered the build file, but the source compile is progressing normally.

`dbg` built successfully and the normal asset cook step passed. I’m continuing through `fin` and `opt` so the pushed change has the same coverage as the recent convergence checks.

`fin` is still compiling cleanly. The touched header sits in a shared asset path, so the rebuild fan-out is expected rather than a sign of extra source churn.

The `opt` build is in its final target set. After that I’ll run `ctest -C dbg`, `fin`, and `opt`, then the isolated two-root cooker smokes required for this repo.

All three `ctest` configs passed. I’m checking the cooker command-line shape now so the isolated two-root smoke uses the repo’s required empty asset-type argument instead of a literal `auto`.

The full build/test matrix passed, and the two-root cooker smoke passed for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt` with empty `--asset-type` selecting auto. I’m doing the final status/diff check now, then I’ll stage only the source header and push `main`.

Final diff check is clean. The only intended source change is `core/assets/asset_ref.h`; `.cozter` remains unstaged and will stay out of the commit.

Committed the scoped source cleanup. I’m pushing `main` now, then I’ll confirm the branch state and report the exact validation set.

Removed the remaining concrete backward-compat/convenience surface I found: `AssetRef<T>` no longer accepts raw `CompactString` text via constructor or setter in [core/assets/asset_ref.h](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/assets/asset_ref.h). Existing source already uses typed `Name`/`AssetRef` paths, so this compiled cleanly.

Committed and pushed to `origin/main`:

`75b12da Remove AssetRef text compatibility overloads`

Validation passed:

`cmake --build __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 --config dbg|fin|opt`  
`ctest --test-dir __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 -C dbg|fin|opt --output-on-failure`  
two-root `resource_cooker` smoke for `dbg`, `fin`, `opt`

Working tree is synced with `origin/main`; only the pre-existing `.cozter` local files remain modified.

and I want you to check and fix for:
- fix all bug or error
- follow standard.md rules
- check if all functions are working correctly
- use aligned float if you can
- optimize all the implementations
- redesign for hacky code
- remove duplications
- remove backward compatibilities
- wrong domain

repeat until you no longer find source to change

Using `github:yeet` for the commit/push boundary, while keeping the workflow direct to `main` as in the prior turns. I’ll treat this as an evidence-backed source sweep: inspect standards, scan non-`3rd_parties` code, patch concrete defects or clear maintainability/performance problems, validate, then commit and push only if there are real source changes.

The publish skill normally requires `gh`, but the established workflow in this checkout is direct `main` pushes and the local memory says `gh` is unavailable. I’ll keep the useful parts of that workflow, especially scoped staging and validation, and skip PR-only requirements.

I’m moving through broad text scans first: compatibility markers, hack/workaround language, direct CRT or console use that conflicts with the standard, domain-sensitive text handling, and obvious repeated lookup/allocation patterns. Anything that is only a comment or a harmless local idiom stays untouched.

The first pass did not expose obvious removable legacy shims; most “fallback” hits are current validity fallbacks, not backward compatibility. I found a couple of better candidates to inspect: debug-name/domain text uses, shader/text canonicalization boundaries, and places where source structs still use unaligned `Float3U` but later get SIMD-loaded in tight loops.

I found one concrete correctness issue in the Vulkan state tracker: texture state tracking accepts subresource ranges, but the implementation stores a single state per texture and ignores mip/slice in `getTextureSubresourceState`. I’m going to fix that with per-subresource keys and keep whole-range barriers when all tracked subresources share the same old state.

The patch now stores tracked texture states per `(texture, mip, array slice)`. It still emits a single barrier for a range when all subresources share one old state, and only splits barriers when the range is genuinely mixed. Next I’m compiling this before continuing the sweep.

The first compile caught a privacy issue in my helper: it was reaching into `Texture` private members from a free function. I’m keeping the helper, but passing the image handle and format from the `CommandList` member where that access is already allowed.

A second correctness issue is adjacent: `setPermanentTextureState` and `setPermanentBufferState` only marked the state as permanent; they did not place the transition barrier promised by the API comments. I’m changing those wrappers to transition first, then mark the permanent state.

I’m also fixing a wrong-domain ray tracing lookup: shader table exports were stored as `Name`, which canonicalizes text and can make case-sensitive shader entry defaults compare case-insensitively. I’m switching those export identifiers to exact `AString`/`AStringView` handling so default entry-name exports keep their original spelling.

The `dbg` build is clean after the fixes. I’m doing another pass now over the same categories, with extra focus on remaining canonicalized external text, compatibility shims, direct standard violations, and similar state-tracking bugs.

Another state-tracking pass exposed an API stub that was only recording UAV-barrier preferences and never applying them. I’m wiring those preferences into repeated UAV state use: transition barriers still happen, and same-state UAV barriers are emitted by default unless explicitly disabled per resource.

I’m continuing from the current source diff rather than restarting the sweep. The next checkpoint is to inspect the four touched source files for correctness/style, normalize their line endings back to the repository convention, then run the full verification matrix before staging anything.

The current diff is centered on two real issues: Vulkan texture state tracking was not subresource-accurate, and ray tracing shader exports were going through `Name`, which can cross the external-domain boundary incorrectly. I’m checking the patch against nearby APIs now before widening the scan again.

The API comments confirm one of the fixes: permanent-state calls promise to place the transition barrier before marking the resource permanent, which the old implementation was not doing. I’m widening the sweep again around domain boundaries, standard-rule violations, and suspicious compatibility markers to see whether any independent fixes remain.

The second sweep did not surface another concrete source fix: direct CRT use is confined to wrapper definitions, header loops are normal sentinel/iterator loops, and the “fallback” hits are current behavior or tests rather than legacy shims. I’m normalizing the touched files back to CRLF now so the final diff follows the repo’s line-ending rules.

The touched files are back to CRLF and `git diff --check` is clean for them. I’m moving into validation now: rebuild and run the explicit Linux `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt` test matrix before deciding whether the source diff is commit-ready.

`dbg` rebuilt and all five tests passed. I’m running the same build plus `ctest -C` sequence for `fin` and `opt` now so this does not rely on only one configuration.

`fin` and `opt` both rebuilt, and their `ctest` runs passed. I’m checking the resource cooker command-line shape now so I can run the required isolated two-root smokes using an empty asset-type argument for auto selection.

The isolated two-root cooker smokes passed for `dbg`, `fin`, and `opt` with empty `--asset-type`, each reporting two roots and 34 cooked files. I’m doing the final tree and diff checks now, then I’ll stage only the four source files if everything still looks scoped.

The final targeted repeat scan is clean: no remaining ray-tracing export `Name` paths, no whole-texture-only state tracker entries, and no actionable legacy/fuzzy-compatibility markers in the scoped source. I’m staging just the four source files now.

The index contains only the intended source files; `.cozter` remains unstaged. Cached whitespace checks are clean, so I’m committing this scoped fix and pushing `main`.

Done. Committed and pushed to `main`:

`0a882a9 Fix Vulkan state tracking and shader export domains`

Changed:
- Kept ray-tracing shader export names as exact `AString` / `AStringView` domain text, avoiding `Name` canonicalization: [common.h](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/graphics/common.h:2696), [vulkan_raytracing.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_raytracing.cpp:943)
- Made Vulkan texture state tracking subresource-accurate instead of whole-texture keyed: [vulkan_backend.h](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_backend.h:1342)
- Fixed mixed-subresource barriers, UAV same-state barriers, and permanent-state transition behavior: [vulkan_state_tracking.cpp](/home/limstudio/WorkStation/NWBLot/core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_state_tracking.cpp:135)

Verified:
- `git diff --check`
- `cmake --build __cmake/build/linux-clang-x64 --config dbg|fin|opt`
- `ctest -C dbg|fin|opt`
- Isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes for `impl/assets` + `Testbed/assets`, with empty `--asset-type`, for `dbg|fin|opt`

I repeated the source scans after the fixes and did not find another concrete source change to make. `main` is synced with `origin/main`; only the pre-existing `.cozter` local files remain unstaged.
make code to follow follow standard.md rules

I’ll focus this pass on concrete `standard.md` violations in non-`3rd_parties` code, not broad style churn. First I’m reloading the rules and checking the current tree so any edits stay scoped.

The checkout is still source-clean except for the known `.cozter` local files. I have the first half of `standard.md`; I’m reading the remaining rules, then I’ll run targeted scans that map directly to those rules.

The standard has several measurable rules, so I’m checking those first: line endings/EOF shape, direct CRT or console APIs, qualifier spacing, lock aliases, and namespace/helper patterns. That should surface real violations without reformatting code wholesale.

So far the mechanical checks are mostly clean: source line endings are CRLF, direct CRT/console hits are confined to the wrapper macro definitions, and `enum class` did not show up in project code. I’m tightening the scans around declarations and class layout now, because the first pass included expected false positives.

I found a concrete standard violation now: several override declarations omit the required `virtual` spelling. I’m opening those headers to patch only the affected declarations, while leaving base pure-virtual declarations alone.

I’m editing three headers: `FunctionalInitializerable` overrides, `BackendContext` interface overrides, and one multiline deformable-geometry codec override. Afterward I’ll normalize those files back to CRLF and rerun the standard scans.

`apply_patch` mixed line endings in the touched headers, as expected in this repo. I’m normalizing only those touched files back to CRLF before doing the next checks.

The override pass is now clean except for a known false positive on a multiline declaration whose opening line already contains `virtual`. I’m doing one more standards pass on higher-risk rules before building: scratch allocator use for local temporary containers, direct standard-library wrappers, and unsafe style patterns.

The remaining hits in these scans are expected: wrapper definitions, aliases in global wrapper headers, enum namespaces, and function-local containers that either escape the immediate scope or are broader refactors. I’m going to build the Linux debug config now to catch any header-level issues from the override changes.

Debug build passed, including the automatic graphics asset cook that runs as part of the build. I’m running the debug test set next, then I’ll decide whether the remaining validation needs the full config matrix.

Debug tests passed. Because the changed headers are shared by multiple targets and this repository’s Linux verification is config-specific, I’m going to run `fin` and `opt` builds/tests too before committing.

`fin` build and tests are clean. I’m running the same for `opt`; after that I’ll do the final style/status checks and commit only the intended header changes.

`opt` build and tests passed as well. I’m checking the cooker CLI shape before the isolated two-root smoke runs, because this project uses an empty asset-type argument for auto selection.

summary: Ran another full source-wide optimization sweep and made a small, evidence-backed runtime hot-path cleanup, then normalized the touched files back to CRLF after `apply_patch` introduced mixed line endings. Changes were limited to: `impl/ecs_graphics/deformable_runtime_mesh_cache.cpp` (skip active/stale hash tracking when no runtime cache exists), `impl/ecs_graphics/deformer_system.cpp` (skip live-handle tracking when no runtime resources exist), `impl/ecs_graphics/renderer_system.cpp` (collapse a double map lookup in a render-path log), and `core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_resource_bindings.cpp` (use `insert_or_assign` instead of `operator[]` assignment for descriptor heap lookup). Verified with the full Linux `dbg|fin|opt` build + `ctest` matrix and isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes; all passed. Committed and pushed `ccd52c0` to `origin/main`; `main` is synced and only pre-existing local `.cozter` files remain dirty/unstaged.
long_term: Application entrypoints/tests must use `NWB_DEFINE_APPLICATION_ENTRY_POINT`, initialize `NWB::Core::Common::InitializerGuard` before any startup/CLI work (including Windows wide-argv conversion), be nesting-safe, return `-1` on startup failure (including `CommandLineToArgvW` failure), ensure any synthetic fallback `argv` is null-terminated, and link direct executable/test targets with `nwb_common` before `nwb_alloc`.

Non-`3rd_parties` code follows `.helper/standard.md` and `.gitattributes`: use CRT wrapper macros over direct CRT calls, preserve CRLF/EOF conventions, avoid side-effect loop conditions in shared headers, and keep `RuntimeException` in `global/type.h` as `std::runtime_error`.

Repo-wide hardening/optimization should be evidence-backed, minimal-churn, repeated until no concrete issues remain, never create empty commits, and keep `.cozter` artifacts out of commits unless explicitly requested.
Logger global registration must use RAII guards in `logger/client/logger.h` and `logger/server/server.h`, and those guard types must remain `final`, non-copyable, and non-movable.

Shader entry-point naming is external-domain text: preserve exact strings end-to-end for SPIR-V/Vulkan lookup (no canonicalizing and no fuzzy/case-insensitive matching), with caches keyed by exact entry text.

Vulkan/graphics backend must use centralized descriptor/layout helpers, destroy owned layouts in `core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_resource_bindings.cpp`, handle aspect bits correctly (single-aspect `VkBufferImageCopy`, never apply `VK_IMAGE_ASPECT_COLOR_BIT` to stencil-only formats), treat extension domains correctly (`VK_EXT_debug_utils` instance vs `VK_EXT_debug_marker` device), enable marker paths only when required entry points are available, keep extension lookup tables in `core/graphics/vulkan/vulkan_backend_context.h` as shared `static constexpr` `s_` tables, and use shared helpers for correct block-compressed texture block width/height (rectangular ASTC included).
In `dbg` builds only, loader flag `--gpudbg` (default off) may enable graphics debug runtime before `Frame::init()` via existing backend instance-parameter flow.

Asset paths must reject virtual-path override metadata via `Core::Assets::RejectVirtualPathOverrideField` in `core/assets/asset_paths.h`, and filesystem segment transfer stays split into typed read/write helpers with `StagedDirectoryCleanupGuard` owning log strings and implementation using `GlobalFilesystemDetail::FileStream`/`StreamOffset` aliases.

Math uses column-vector semantics (`M * v`) with translation in `r[3]`; math parity tests in `tests/math/math_tests.cpp` must stay synchronized; prefer `simdmath` with canonical `math/type.h` storage (`Float2U`, `Float3U`, `Float4U`, aligned `Float4`) and keep shader-like helpers GLSL-aligned while ensuring `NWB_INLINE` expands to `inline` in `dbg`.

Camera behavior is centralized in `core/scene`: camera validity requires finite transform data (including scale), unit finite quaternion rotation, and valid projection data, with fallback to the first valid camera and consumers using scene-provided projection data.
`Core::ECS::World::view<T...>()` is read-only enumeration logic and must not allocate missing component pools.

Deformable asset/codec/cooker/runtime-editing paths stay strongly typed (required `asset.index_type` and streams), strictly reject malformed/degenerate/non-finite data, require strict hit identity/revision checks, prefer `ScratchArena` for hot-path temporaries, and centralize frame/tangent resolution via `impl/ecs_graphics/deformable_runtime_helpers.h` semantics.

Backward-compatibility cleanup is intentional, so legacy volume-segment naming support, the singular `IDevice::executeCommandList` path, and the removed global `Mutex` compatibility alias must not be reintroduced.

Linux convergence verification is anchored at `__cmake/build/linux-clang-x64`, requires explicit `ctest -C dbg|fin|opt`, and includes isolated two-root `resource_cooker` smokes for `impl/assets` plus `Testbed/assets` (single-root runs are insufficient, and cooker “auto” selection uses an empty asset-type argument rather than a literal `auto`).
The GitHub CLI (`gh`) is not available in this environment, so GitHub flows that require `gh` may need direct `git` commands unless `gh` is installed.
